_title: I Did Not Come Here To Fit Inside This Frequency
_raw_content: You were not born dysregulated. You were born vast and then handed a nervous system blueprint that belonged to someone else's fear. Every time you feel alien here, it is not because something is wrong with you. It is because your body still remembers a frequency this world has not caught up to. The discomfort was never the problem. The shrinking was.
slides: I did not come here to regulate down to a frequency that was never mine.
My nervous system is not broken. It was built for a signal this world forgot how to carry.
I stop apologizing for the depth of my wiring.
The discomfort I feel in small rooms is not anxiety. It is remembrance.
I was not too much. I was surrounded by systems running on too little.
My body does not need to be fixed. It needs to be believed.
I release every blueprint I inherited that taught me my aliveness was dangerous.
caption:
You spent years thinking your intensity was the wound.

It was the gift.

The wound was every moment you were taught to dim it. To regulate yourself into a shape your parents could manage. To call your depth a disorder and your sensitivity a flaw.

Your nervous system was never malfunctioning.
It was receiving more than your environment could hold.

So it learned to shut down. Contract. Freeze. Perform calm instead of feel alive.

This is your permission to stop shrinking.

Not to explode. Not to bypass. But to finally let your body take up the space it was always wired for.

Save this for the days you forget you were built for more than survival.
